channel.title: Linear algebra on James Akl
channel.description: Recent content in Linear algebra on James Akl

item.title: Matrix Calculus Notation
item.description: This article discusses notational practices in matrix calculus and recommends a mnemonic inspired by the outer product. Background and Motivation In the context of differentiation, Leibniz’s notation ${\big(\frac{\mathrm{d}f}{\mathrm{d}x}\big)}$ is typically used for functions ${f: \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}}$, i.e., scalar-valued function of a real variable, and is a shorthand representing ${\lim_{\Delta x \to 0} \frac{\Delta f}{\Delta x}}$. In higher-dimensional settings, a function can be multivariate (multidimensional input), vector-valued (multidimensional output), or both.
